cause: spec-judge round 1 returned needs-input: two frozen narrowings were in flight without a ledger record, and the conductor had wrongly cleared one contradicting pair by reading two identically-scoped When steps as different operations. The judge also found the procedural anti-pattern and the single-brancher wrong subject stated in prose with no scenario exercising either.
resolution: Recorded both Clearance grants in the ledger with the owner's in-session verdict; narrowed the producer's reports-complete Given under the second grant, which also closed a pre-existing contradiction against the frozen entangled-Given scenario; added procedural and single-brancher scenarios to both suites plus a negative guard that an already-loseable dimension is left alone.

cause: spec-judge round 2 returned ALIGNED but surfaced an architect observation the bar could not survive: the pairwise rule as authored would over-fire on a convention the corpus relies on everywhere — a general scenario and a specific sibling whose narrower Given carves out an exception, where the general Given does not literally exclude it. Read literally, the new rule flags those pairs as defects.
resolution: Added the specialization clause to the rule in every home (the suite-format bar and its reference node, the default judge, the ACED validator, the producer procedure) and froze it in both suites as a negative guard. A contradiction is a pair with no intended winner — the Conflict floor's own definition — so the rule now inherits that qualifier instead of contradicting it. Consequence worth naming: under this clause the second Clearance was not strictly required, since the convention already permitted the implicit reading; the narrowing stands as a clarity improvement the owner granted, not as a defect repair.
